bead.title: emit structured lock acquire/release events with duration for index.lock and tracker.lock
bead.description:
PROBLEM
DDx has no visibility into how long workers hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock. Operators diagnosing tracker-lock-timeout errors must infer from filesystem mtimes and ps output instead of structured logs. Lock-lifetime cap enforcement (separate child) depends on having this signal first.

ROOT CAUSE
Lock acquisition sites in cli/cmd/work.go, cli/cmd/run.go, and cli/internal/bead/store.go log nothing structured around acquire/release. Executing agent must locate the wrappers introduced in [[scope-locks-to-mutation-window]] (or the existing call sites if that child has not landed yet) and wrap them in an instrumented helper.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small instrumented-lock helper in cli/internal/lockmetrics/ (or extend an existing util package) that wraps acquire/release and emits one JSON-line event per acquire and release containing: lock_name, acquired_at (RFC3339Nano), released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation (e.g. 'execute-bead.stage-commit', 'bead.update'). Events go through the existing ddx logs/event stream (consistent with .ddx/attachments/ event conventions).

NON-SCOPE
- Enforcing a hold-time cap (separate child)
- Refactoring lock acquisition scope itself (separate child)
- Metrics for non-git locks (worktree locks, etc.)
- Dashboards or aggregation tooling.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestLockMetrics_EmitsAcquireAndReleaseEvents asserts that acquiring and releasing the wrapped lock produces two JSON events with required fields (lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation).
2. TestLockMetrics_DurationMatchesElapsed asserts duration_ms equals released_at minus acquired_at within 5ms tolerance.
3. TestLockMetrics_VisibleInDdxLogs asserts the events surface via `ddx logs` (or whatever the existing structured log accessor is) for a sample execute-bead run.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/lockmetrics/... ./cmd/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
